Starring David Caruso, Jonathan Togo and Jessy Schram(n)

Meanwhile, Tony fears Ziva may not be loyal to the team, and a psychologist uncovers the truth about the relationship between Gibbs and Agent Macy. With Chris O'Donnell, Louise Lombard and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The team investigates when an Islamic terrorist group in Iraq steals a truck with $5million and track the money back to America. The agents learn the cash has been used to set up a bank robbery intended as a ruse to allow a bank employee to transfer $100million of the terrorists' money that has been frozen by the government in an offshore bank. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell and LL Cool J(n)

programme.title: Matlock
programme.desc: Michelle is taken hostage by a man who blames Matlock for his brother's suicide(n)

programme.title: Perry Mason

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Lt Dean captures Gibbs in Mexico and hands him over to Paloma Reynosa, head of a drug cartel and daughter of the man he killed. She tries to persuade him to aid her master plan by threatening to kill his father. Alejandro Rivera arrives with a platoon of Mexican troops, but hopes of rescue may be premature. Meanwhile, Ziva is accepted as a US citizen(n)

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Part two of two. The team tries to provide the evidence needed to convict a serial killer but faces a series of challenges and struggles to reach the courtroom in time(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team is called in to investigate when three dehydrated survivors from a helicopter crash are discovered. The pilot is initially missing, but his body later washes ashore with a gunshot wound to the head. Things get complicated when one of the crew members tells the agents the chopper was hit by another aircraft, while another states that the pilot tried to kill them all by crashing into the sea on purpose. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: Calvin Lee, an executive naval officer, appears to commit suicide by hanging himself from a public road bridge. When examining CCTV footage of the incident, Sam and Callen discover the dead man tossed away a small object before jumping to his death, and when they learn the late lieutenant commander had been working on a sensitive project involving new cloaking technology for submarines, they immediately take an interest in the item. The investigation draws the team into the furtive world of LA's Chinese community, where they discover Lee might have been harbouring some personal as well as professional secrets. Crime drama, starring Chris O'Donnell(n)

programme.title: Scorpion
programme.desc: Hackers seize control of the US Navy's aircraft and warships, turning their weapons at American cities. Return of the high-octane thriller, starring Elyes Gabriel(n)

programme.title: Soldier, Soldier
programme.desc: A remote British-ruled island in the South Pacific is invaded and the King's Own Fusiliers find themselves part of the task force sent to retake it. Unfortunately, Corporal Hobbs is soon captured and tortured by enemy troops, and Colonel Eastwood's daring plan of attack has explosive consequences(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Having discovered Irene Radford is back, Gina is determined to put her old enemy behind bars once and for all - and the two are soon face to face again when an intruder at the Radford house turns out to be Irene herself, who claims she has returned because she is critically ill with cancer. However, the police have got wind that she is plotting one final raid before retiring from a life of crime for good, so Phil is tasked to monitor a prison meeting between Irene and her son David, where the top brass are hoping he'll glean valuable information about the job. Unfortunately, the philandering DS is easily distracted by an old flame working at the prison and misses half the conversation - so when the raid gets under way, it soon becomes clear they've been duped, and a senior officer ends up staring down the barrel of Irene's gun. Back at the station, Honey is upset to learn that Amber has taken the 'shy and sensitive' Steve Hunter's virginity, while Sam seems to be warming to Phil. Is ro(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: On a busy night behind the bar, Mickie notices Jodie is becoming breathless and feverish, and tries to persuade her to seek medical help. Tricia and Mark find working together tense, but agree to be as professional as possible, while Diane suffers from pre-wedding jitters and turns to Ric for support. Her fears may be well-founded, as Owen's stag night ends with him kissing Chrissie(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A young rollerblade hockey player is coy about how he received his mysterious injuries, Liz's boyfriend leaves her for another woman, and Tina acts beyond the call of duty when she contacts Social Services about an injured boy(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Following the Radford family's botched attempt to spring David from prison, Irene drives off with Gina at gunpoint and forces the inspector to let her colleagues know she means business. If they don't give up the chase, she won't hesitate to pull the trigger. Soon, Gina finds herself alone with her enemy, and as they begin to talk, the two of them realise they understand one another better than they could possibly have imagined. However, when Irene orders the shooting of Jack Gaunt, it becomes clear Gina is still in very real danger. Meanwhile, Honey has it out with Steve, disappointed that he had sex with Amber, and accusing him of being as bad as his brother. Indeed, Phil is having his fair share of problems as Sam discovers that his New Year's invitation followed hard on the heels of his dalliance with the prison liaison officer. Roberta Taylor, Lynda Bellingham, Lisa Maxwell and Scott Maslen star(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Pete takes time off to treat Christine to a day at the races, Mandy is the subject of gossip in the Square, and Dot interferes in Mark's life and gives Hattie shocking new(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: PatCabs needs another driver, Rachel and Kathy grab some bargains at the jumble sale, and Michelle gets a job offer. Sharon is impressed with Grant's hard work at the Vic(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Blue Watch are called to a DHSS office where a distressed claimant has covered himself in petrol and set himself alight. Starring Glen Murphy(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: While searching a Cambridge antiques fair for religious books with Charlotte and Charlie Gimbert, Lovejoy discovers a rare Bible that seems to offer a solution to all his problems. Meanwhile, Tinker has a hard time persuading Beth's father that she should carry on working for such a rogue. Drama, guest starring Richard Briers and James Ellis, with Ian McShane(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim goes against his better judgement to help Philippa Vale - aka the Ice Maiden - discover who is trying to kill her. Drama, guest starring Liza Goddard, with John Nettles and Terence Alexander(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Freddie loses a valuable ring, while Jack, who found it and tried to pawn it, is filled with remorse. Meanwhile, Adrian shocks the family with his radical change in personality, insisting on being called Jimmy. Comedy, starring Jonathon Morris, Ronald Forfar, Victor McGuire and Jean Boht(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: One of the yellowcoat's professionalism is questioned by a sharp-eyed passer-by who spots her entering a staff chalet late at night. With Simon Cadell, Ruth Madoc and Gillian Taylforth(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Jean accepts Lionel's proposal, but their happiness is not shared by everyone. Sandy walks out on her boyfriend while Stephen tries to organise a surprise party for Penny(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: After recruitment for his Merry Men hits an all-time low, a letter from an enthusiast with excellent credentials gets Billy excited again, and he organises a committee to greet the newest member. Long-running comedy, starring Keith Clifford and Peter Sallis(n)

programme.title: Judge John Deed
programme.desc: Jo Mills takes on the case of a worker killed on a building site, convinced that the managing director of the company should be held accountable for his neglect of health and safety legislation. However, the Lord Chancellor's department once again try to divert the course of justice to avoid embarrassment - and Deed's ex-wife is appointed counsel for the defence. Legal drama, starring Martin Shaw and Jenny Seagrove(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The discovery of a bloodstained pair of hot pants results in the team being asked to reinvestigate the 1971 disappearance of a mother and her six-month-old son. Lane, who worked on the original case, quickly identifies the victim's husband as the chief suspect, but when Halford and Standing learn two other women went missing in the area on the same day, they suspect they are looking for a serial killer. Amanda Redman stars(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: A war begins between rival taxi firms, confronting the team with a series of coincidences to investigate, and Taggart's family problems are compounded when his daughter is implicated in a murder investigation. Glasgow-set detective drama, with Mark McManus, James MacPherson and Blythe Duff. Directed by series regular Alan Macmillan, who also worked on The Bill and Ballykissangel. The supporting cast includes Iain Anders, Robert Robertson, Stewart Preston, Harriet Buchan, Leigh Biagi, Andy McEwan and Peter O'Brien(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: As the day of the wedding dawns, Chrissie and Owen wake up together. But while she is convinced this means he won't go through with marrying Diane, she is soon disabused of this notion when he dismisses the previous night as a drunken mistake. Tricia ends up becoming everybody's confidante, and Jodie is rushed to hospital after she collapses at Mickie's house. Connie discovers her condition is caused by a disastrous old operation, and ends up having to remove her lung. The tense situation is complicated further when it emerges Jodie and Will have a history together(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Jack has a crisis of confidence when a patient arrives in the department and leaves Charlie to pick up the pieces. Meanwhile, a teenage girl samples drugs with an older man(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Staff shortages cause tension in CPU, with Ramani bearing the brunt as she struggles to cope with an increased workload - and when an outside source brings the matter to Adam's attention, the situation becomes hugely political, only serving to aggravate the already volatile relationship between Jack and Neil. By way of an immediate solution, Jack assigns Terry to help Ramani with the case of a man suspected of being a paedophile - but Ramani is worried about Terry's ability to deal with such a task given his own traumatic background. Meanwhile, the rest of the relief let their hair down at Lance's stag party - but when the guest of honour nips outside for a breath of fresh air, he is suddenly attacked from behind by a group who put a bag over his head and bundle him into the back of a van. It could just be a stag night prank - or it could be something more serious. Guest starring Annabelle Apsion and Colm O'Maonlai(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mandy tries to persuade Mark to let her stay in the squat. Ian's dishonesty catches up with him, while Arthur plays detective to track down his garden gnome(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Two birthdays in the Fowler family; Pete is the life and soul of Martin's party, but will an unwelcome guest ruin Michelle's?(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: A window cleaner gets his arm trapped in a cradle mechanism at the top of a tower block, and Sicknote is sent over the edge to rescue him(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: A romantic weekend in the country turns sour for Charlotte and Lovejoy when one of her friends takes a shine to him. Matters deteriorate further for the dodgy dealer when he learns Charlie is trying to evict his business. Light-hearted drama, starring Ian McShane, Caroline Langrishe and Malcolm Tierney(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim experiences complications while conducting an investigation into the disappearance of a financier. John Nettles stars(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Lilo Lill turns up on the doorstep, Joey meets Roxy, Adrian buys something really stupid and Aveline is insulted by a photographer(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: The staff try to stop Ted celebrating his birthday every two weeks in a bid to con gifts and free drinks out of gullible holiday-makers. Meanwhile, there may be trouble ahead when the runner-up in the Holiday Princess competition is spotted entering a staff chalet at night. Comedy, starring Paul Shane and Su Pollard(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: The announcement of Jean and Lionel's wedding date gives the neighbourhood women the perfect opportunity to go shopping for new outfits - until the bride-to-be is accused of shoplifting. Comedy, with Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Lenny hears a voice from above, and decides he must wait patiently for more information, leaving the gang to make sure he is well-equipped to receive messages. Comedian Bobby Ball returns as the gullible pickle factory worker, with Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton(n)

programme.title: Hetty Wainthropp Investigates
programme.desc: The sleuth tries to help a road rage victim, but lands right in the middle of a family tragedy she may be unable to control. Hetty tries to fix the missing pieces of the puzzle into place and her client gets a very unexpected result(n)

programme.title: Ms Fisher's Modern Murder Mysteries
programme.desc: Part one of two. After two pop singers are electrocuted while performing on a TV music show, Peregrine's impartiality is tested when an old boyfriend is named as the lead suspect in the case. Geraldine Hakewill stars(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The squad is ordered by its new boss to look into the unsolved murder of a barrister found bound and gagged in his car 25 years ago. The case was a rare failure for a former chief inspector, although his notes point to a very clear suspect. As they uncover the late advocate's unusual hobbies, they gradually learn exactly what happened. Anita Dobson, Jenny Agutter and Keith Barron guest star(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: An informant is due to give evidence against his former boss, one of Glasgow's most powerful gang leaders - but police fail to get the witness to court. Drama, with Mark McManus, Blythe Duff and James MacPherson. Co-star Gray O'Brien makes one of six appearances in the series, years before playing Tony Gordon in Coronation Street. More recently he popped up in The Loch, and Shakespeare & Hathaway: Private Investigators. Director Richard Holthouse went on to helm several episodes of Midsomer Murders(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jess goes in for a routine scan, which exposes how many weeks pregnant she is. When she begins bleeding, Mubbs is forced to reveal this information in surgery - which in turn proves that Sean cannot be the father. Mark considers resuming his relationship with Tricia, although Chrissie isn't sure it's a wise move. Mickie pressures Jodie for the truth, and the resulting revelation prompts Will to consider resigning, until an incident involving a patient convinces him otherwise - but Jodie is still plotting revenge(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: A wife and her mother-in-law fall out, and one is badly burned in the ensuing fracas. A bisexual man, now living with his girlfriend, is stalked by a male former lover - who he runs over. Meanwhile, relatives of the woman wrongly declared as dead complain to Elliot, and both Jack and Charlie have to make statements(n)

programme.title: The Bill
programme.desc: Lance is missing on his wedding day, as friends try to 'cure' him of his homosexuality. Terry tries to prove his suspicions that brother Ben is a child abuser, while no-nonsense new DC Jo Masters arrives at Sun Hill to defuse the tension between Jack and Neil - but proves to be their worst nightmare(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Mandy hassles Pete to get a job, and Dot makes a shocking discovery. Grant disappears, leaving Phil and Sharon alone in the Queen Vic to face an uninvited midnight visitor(n)

programme.title: Classic EastEnders
programme.desc: Dot tries to talk to Mark about Arthur's friendship with Mrs Hewitt. Rachel puts a spanner in Frank's plans to relocate PatCabs, and Sharon and Phil are still alone in the Vicc(n)

programme.title: London's Burning
programme.desc: Fresh young recruit Kate Stevens joins the team and makes a great impression by saving a child involved in an accident involving two cars and a chemical lorry(n)

programme.title: Lovejoy
programme.desc: Charlotte buys an 18th-century portrait at auction, but on returning home is attacked by a robber, who steals the prestigious item. Looking out the window, she sees Lovejoy's pick-up speeding away. When quizzed, the dealer finds the events of that night difficult to recall as he had been seriously inebriated. Ian McShane and Caroline Langrishe star(n)

programme.title: Bergerac
programme.desc: Jim enters the world of secret handshakes and rolled-up trouser legs to investigate the mystery surrounding a disastrous Masonic initiation ceremony. However, as he probes the case, his relationship with Susan begins to show signs of strain. Drama, starring John Nettles and Louise Jameson(n)

programme.title: Bread
programme.desc: Aveline is admitted to hospital and Adrian becomes a gardener to an unusual and overpowering lady. When he quits, Jack decides to take up her special offer(n)

programme.title: Hi-de-Hi!
programme.desc: Resident Punch and Judy man Mr Partridge cons good-natured Jeffrey into funding one of his infamous drunken binges. Holiday camp capers, starring Simon Cadell, Leslie Dwyer, Paul Shane and Ruth Madoc(n)

programme.title: As Time Goes By
programme.desc: Lionel gets cold feet as the wedding approaches, while Jean tries to remain calm despite her sister-in-law's interference. Comedy, starring Judi Dench and Geoffrey Palmer(n)

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine
programme.desc: Clegg takes care of a friendly dog for the day, but when Truly, Billy and Alvin visit, the canine ferociously guards his new minder's house, leaving the gang to spend a day on the doorstep thinking up ambitious plans to negotiate their entry. Comedy, starring Peter Sallis and Frank Thornton(n)

programme.title: The Inspector Lynley Mysteries
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Lynley returns to his ancestral home to celebrate his engagement, but 20 years of anger over his mother's indiscretion with a doctor makes the occasion unpleasant. A murder in a nearby village provides him with a temporary distraction - until mounting evidence appears to suggest his brother is implicated. Mystery drama, starring Nathaniel Parker(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The unsolved-crime squad reopens the case of an Asian woman who was attacked eight years ago, and has been in a coma ever since. After enlisting the aid of a community liaison officer, it becomes apparent there is conflict in the victim's family, as well as a possible connection with the disappearance of a private detective. Nina Wadia, Keith Allen and Navin Chowdhry guest star(n)

programme.title: Taggart
programme.desc: Feature-length episode. The strange activities of a religious cult prompt Jardine and Reid to investigate a series of murders committed by a killer obsessed with the biblical plagues of Egypt. Glasgow-set detective drama, starring Blythe Duff and James MacPherson(n)

programme.title: Sblij a Sbloj
programme.desc: The monsters are busy helping at the office, but somehow manage to lose the letter y(n)

programme.desc: Fun and adventures with Glan and Mostyn, two koala brothers who travel around in a yellow aeroplane(n)

programme.desc: Bach and Lleucu race around the garden in their new fast cars(n)

programme.title: Sion y Chef

Coverage of the fourth-round replay, which comes from Kassam Stadium, as the teams contest the right to face West Bromwich Albion at The Hawthorns in the next round. The match at St James' Park ended 0-0, with Newcastle forward Joelinton having two goals disallowed for offside, and League One Oxford will now feel they have an excellent chance of producing an upset against their Premier League opponents(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators are on the case of a locksmith who conned a London hospital out of almost half a million pounds by over-charging for supplies(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Crewe, Cheshire, Blackwell in the West Midlands, and Poplar in east London(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Anna Larkin thinks Australia will offer her son the perfect lifestyle, but husband Aaron is moving nowhere until they have spent a trial week in Brisbane. With Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The team catch up with a second-hand car dealer who sold a potentially deadly vehicle, and track down a fish merchant who has been illegally harvesting shellfish(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire, before seeing if they make profits at auction. Anita Manning presents(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery. Daniel attends a restaurant run by prison inmates and has to help chaplain Cathy overcome her crisis of faith.(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction(n)

programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Flower farmer Shelley Sishton and sweet pea grower Darren Everest compete at the Kent County Show. Their displays are bespoke and spectacular as they go all out for a gold card(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin-Sharp and Raj Bisram continue their tour of Kent as they search for items to sell at an auction in Colchester(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Party Political Broadcast
programme.desc: By the Conservative Party(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Presented by Alex Jones and Matt Baker(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Sacha prepares to perform a groundbreaking bowel transplant operation, but Max fears he is the wrong man for the job, given his recent bereavement and past struggles with mental health. Nicky is struggling to keep her debts under control, only to be confronted with her mother as a patient, who reveals that she now owes even more money. Donna encounters a casting director, and tries to get herself on his books(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Jack is rushed to hospital after collapsing at the scene of a car crash, which Thomas suspects could be the result of exposure to the same nerve agent that was used on John Sealy. With Jack's life hanging in the balance, it falls to Nikki, Thomas and Clarissa to find the truth and save their friend(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex
programme.desc: Ayo is desperate to find out why his first love Felicia broke up with him out of the blue, and Michael and Casey wonder if they will ever be able to trust each other again(n)

programme.title: My Mate's a Bad Date
programme.desc: Self-confessed serial dater Tyler wants to find the male version of herself, so Oloni puts that idea to the test as he sets her up on three challenging dates(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic swimming silver medallist Siobhan-Marie O'Connor, England cricketer Jake Ball, Great Britain Fed Cup tennis captain Anne Keothavong and former Wales prop Adam Jones join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators uncover a scandalous abuse of public money when an NHS manager cooks up a plot to steal £250,000 - money meant to pay for dialysis equipment for patients(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: The presenters follow the development of properties in Dalton-Le-Dale in County Durham, Irlam in Manchester and Plumstead in London(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Krystyn Onaivi wants to give her children the sunshine lifestyle she loved as a child by moving to Australia. However, husband Joseph is more than happy with his life in the UK. A trial week in Perth is arranged to give the couple the chance to look at the homes, jobs and lifestyle on offer 'Down Under'. Whether the experience will unite or drive a wedge between the pair remains to be seen. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The officers catch a fly-tipper who dumped tons of waste in the grounds of an Oldham school for children with learning difficulties, leaving the school with a £22,000 clean-up bill(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents the programme from the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with antiques experts Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram assisting the reds and blues(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ruhma deals with a new mother who is convinced her baby has been swapped. Al's views on protecting children online do not go down well with Zara(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: A carpet magnate is killed in an accident at his warehouse, but his youngest daughter suspects foul play and hires Frank and Luella to investigate her own two sisters(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman helps a married couple with a £500,000 budget find a romantic retirement home in the Dorset countryside. Nicki also meets members of the public committed to refurbishing the region's distinctive 'finger posts'(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Dairy farmer Tereza shows her prize goats and aspiring agricultural assessor Mark competes for recognition of his young Southdown flock at the Tendring Hundred Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram go antique shopping in Norfolk, where the items that take their fancy include an unusual gavel, a pair of polo horses and a vintage train set(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Alex Jones present the live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Linda struggles at the support session and breaks down afterwards - and soon enough, she is back on the bottle. Bernadette is curious about Karen's lack of concern when the police update her about Keanu, so she uses her mother's phone to leave a message for her brother. Sharon is taken aback by Dennis's bad attitude towards her, and Patrick, Sheree and Denise celebrate Isaac's new job at Walford High(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: The quarter-finalists prepare for another two challenges but first there's a surprise as, for one week only, Michelin-starred chef Tom Kerridge joins the judges in Angela Hartnett's place. Then it's down to business as Mary Berry asks them to cook their ultimate family pie and Chris Bavin challenges them to rustle up a dish using a root vegetable usually served on the side. As usual, those who least impress the judges compete in the eliminator, a recipe close to Tom's heart that he expects to be executed perfectly. Presented by Claudia Winkleman(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The celebratory launch of a new luxury resort is cut short when a woman is found electrocuted in her hotel bathtub - with the door locked from the inside. It looks like a simple case of suicide, but with Jack Mooney no longer around to confirm Commissioner Selwyn's suspicions, DI Neville Parker is temporarily flown over from Manchester to close the case. However, he soon realises that island life is not for him, so when evidence of suspicious activity turns up, he is dismayed to realise he will have to extend his stay and investigate the case as murder. Ralf Little joins the cast, with guest appearances by Steve Pemberton and Samantha Bond(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Harpenden, St Albans, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Newscast
programme.desc: The name may have changed but it's business as usual as the Brexitcast team - Laura Kuennsberg, Chris Mason, Katya Adler and Adam Fleming - deliver their usual mix of light-hearted gossip and serious analysis about the latest political goings-on(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Sumera Angus is used to getting her own way and firmly believes she and her family will be much happier living in Australia. They spend a week in Brisbane to see what to offer - but it may not be enough to convince cautious husband Murray. Presented by Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram scour Kent for items to sell at auction in Rayleigh, Essex, along the way buying a French sunburst clock and an old school bell(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace presents the first episode of finals week, in which the four remaining contestants must cook for an exclusive gathering of some of the nation's greatest chefs. Each of the contenders takes responsibility for one course of the four-course meal, under the watchful eye of judges Marcus Wareing and Monica Galetti(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: The Super League Show
programme.desc: Tanya Arnold presents action from the opening round of fixtures, including Toronto Wolfpack v Castleford Tigers, St Helens v Salford Red Devils and Leeds Rhinos v Hull FC(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: A Scottish duo look for a rental home on Madeira, with the help of local property expert Cecelia. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team explores the Welsh coast. Nick Crane investigates evidence that a devastating tsunami hit 400 years ago, and finds out why scientists planning a trip to Mars find the local landscape a surprisingly good stand-in for the red planet's surface. Alice Roberts tests the claim that the world's first powered flight was made by a Welsh carpenter seven years before the Wright brothers and Dick Strawbridge reveals how, in 1947, a man on holiday to Anglesey came up with the design for a new car that would conquer the world - the Land Rover(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A look at television and film appearances made over the years by actress Deborah Kerr, capturing the milestones and highlights of her life and career that led to her being considered a Hollywood icon. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Black Narcissus (1947)
programme.desc: British nuns establish a school and hospital on a remote mountainside in the Himalayas, where the nursing sisters face isolation as well as native mistrust. However, it is the presence of a male land agent that takes a final toll on their peace of mind. Oscar-winning drama from directorial team Powell and Pressburger. With Deborah Kerr, David Farrar, Kathleen Byron, Flora Robson and Jean Simmons(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Mark Stacey and Christina Trevanion are in Huddersfield, West Yorkshire, to assess a range of items, while presenter Paul Martin pays a visit to Nostell Priory near Wakefield(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Ho Chi Minh City to Halong Bay, and uncovers the traumatic 20th-century history of Vietnam, a country that went from a French colony to a socialist republic. Along the way, he braves the city streets on a motorcycle, samples French-Vietnamese fusion food, and tries his hand at the national sport(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Comedian Romesh Ranganathan joins the team in the studio. Chris Harris races the £2.5million McLaren Speedtail against the RAF's latest fighter jet, and Paddy McGuinness tries out a cheap but luxurious off-roader(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: A look at the smaller boats that fish off Cornwall's coast, often with only a single crew member. Despite being a sustainable method of fishing, this traditional approach is coming under threat from industrialisation, a lack of new talent taking up the trade, and declining stocks of mackerel and other once common fish. In Penberth, James is the only fisherman under 65 - and since traditional methods rely on boats supporting each other, he may have to give up when the older generation retires. On the Helford River, a veteran fisherman shares his concerns for the fish processing industry if Britain leaving the EU leads to a decline in foreign labour(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Universal Credit from the point of view of both government employees and those who rely on the controversial benefits system to support themselves. The first episode focuses on Peckham Jobcentre in London. Rachel, who left her job to care for her elderly parents, now struggles to support her family on Universal Credit while having to cope with debt and depression. Jobcentre employee Karen finds herself faced with similar difficulties to her clients, and has to take a second job to support herself. The programme also follows the difficulties faced by civil servants in overseeing sweeping changes to the benefits system(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam is feeling the strain of her menopause. After a night out with the girls, she decides a trip to her doctor is in order(n)

programme.title: The Archiveologists
programme.desc: Comedy performers Diane Morgan and Joe Wilkinson delve into the archive to parody a 1970s film on what to do at a business meeting(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's important national and international news stories with Emily Maitlis and Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Body Clock: What Makes Us Tick?
programme.desc: Everyone has an internal biological clock that governs their daily rhythms, including diet, general health and exercise patterns. But is it possible to manage the body clock better? To find out, former commando Aldo Kane is locked in an abandoned nuclear bunker for 10 days with no way of telling the time. He is monitored around the clock by evolutionary biologist Ella Al-Shamahi and a team of scientists as he carries out a barrage of tests to uncover exactly what makes the body clock tick. At the same time, two couples test the latest thinking on how to improve their lives, with general advice on sleeping better and managing their time(n)

programme.title: Monty Don's American Gardens
programme.desc: The host explores the scenery of the west coast of the United States, beginning in Arizona where he learns about cacti and marvels at the sight of them in the desert and in private gardens where they have replaced traditional plants and lawns. In Palm Springs, he views gardens with famous owners, from film stars to big businessmen and politicians. Los Angeles is Monty's next stop where he hears how schoolchildren are taking up gardening. His trip ends in Seattle where he visits a tropical getaway for one of the city's main workforces and views some beautiful green spaces just outside the city(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Criminal barristers Sasha Wass and Jeremy Dein examine the 1935 morphine poisoning of a care home resident Ada Baguley in Nottingham. Initially Ada was thought to have died from natural causes, but a post-mortem revealed her body to contain lethal doses of morphine. Dorothea Waddingham, the owner of the nursing home and primary care giver of Ada, was later convicted of murder(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Crews tend to a 95-year-old Navy veteran as he is winched from the deck of a cruise ship, having lain undiscovered in his cabin for 11 hours. He's diabetic, dehydrated and has suspected acute pancreatitis. Meanwhile. a man has his holiday interrupted with kidney problems. Narrated by Kevin Whately(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram look for bargains in East Anglia, as well as having a go at step dancing and discovering an 18th-century colelction of self-playing instruments(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at some of the funding issues for parliamentary candidates. Plus, the Government's independent enquiry into sex abuse. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley investigate the fake Botox treatment that left one woman with life changing injuries. Elsewhere, a report on false online reviews that are ruining business across the UK, deadly fake camping stoves that can explode when they are lit, and Michelle tests imitation Airpods headphones(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Jo Coburn and guests discuss the big political issues of the day. Includes live coverage of Prime Minister's Questions from midday(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Amanda from Essex wants to swap the cold UK weather for the sunshine of Majorca, so local property expert Jan shows her four rental properties on the Spanish island(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Neil Oliver visits a coastal amphitheatre near Land's End, Cornwall, where he performs an extract from The Tempest and learns how the venue came to be constructed. Nicholas Crane explores the history of a coach company that has transported northerners to the south coast since the 1930s, Miranda Krestovnikoff goes in search of a family of white-beaked dolphins, Adrian Gray demonstrates the art of balancing stones, and Alice Roberts finds out why the sea has its distinctive smell(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by Irish actor Richard Harris, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Cromwell (1970)
programme.desc: The rise of Oliver Cromwell to power during the turbulent years of the mid-17th century, charting his days as an active parliamentarian to his victory over the royal armies in the English Civil War, and his subsequent role as the country's Lord Protector. Historical drama, with Richard Harris, Alec Guinness, Robert Morley, Dorothy Tutin, Frank Finlay, Patrick Magee, Charles Gray and Timothy Dalton(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Glemham Hall in the Suffolk countryside. David Fletcher discovers scripts from the TV series Lovejoy, and Jonathan Pratt spots Beatles dolls(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Dr Maggie Aderin-Pocock, Reverend Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels around South-east Asia, guided by his 1913 Bradshaw's Handbook on a 2,500-mile railway adventure across six countries. In the Annamite Mountains west of Da Nang, Michael joins modern Vietnamese on holiday in a quirky facsimile of a medieval French village. Later, in the tailoring capital of Hoi An, Michael sees how Cham weavers make their distinctive brocade(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Yorkshire Walks
programme.desc: Artist Shanaz Gulzar seeks inspiration while walking a stretch of the 109-mile Cleveland Way, meeting fellow walkers along the way and finding spots to sketch and recite poetry. Starting in Runswick Bay, and armed only with a handheld 360-degree selfie-style camera. she climbs the steep steps to the cliff top before strolling along the coastal path, absorbing the beautiful landscape and its industrial past, before finishing at Whitby for sunset(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: After a positive first couple of weeks, the temptation of unhealthy treats and alcohol is threatening to undo the group's progress, so Tom shares a series of recipes to help the volunteers with their temptations for unhealthy treats. He starts with his own take on Asian crispy duck pancakes, before demonstrating how to make fudge chocolate brownies which are lower in calories. Later, Tom visits Lauren in her home, where he gives her a lesson in making prawn and avocado rice paper rolls which contain lots of fresh, healthy ingredients(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: Michael Mosley tests whether an extra hour a week in nature improves your health, and lain Gregoire investigates an illness that can transform a child's personality overnight. Meanwhile, Giles Yeo investigates whether trendy plant-based milks are any healthier than dairy, and Guddi Singh witnesses pioneering cancer surgery(n)

programme.title: Good Omens
programme.desc: Aziraphale and Crowley's friendship is tested to the limit as their superiors catch up with them. Armageddon starts in earnest, with the Antichrist's powers wreaking havoc across the globe. Dramatisation of Terry Pratchett and Neil Gaiman's fantasy adventure about an angel and a demon joining forces to prevent the end of the world, starring Michael Sheen and David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Stand-up comedian Rob Beckett plays host in this show recorded before a packed house at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience and then introduces routines by rising comedy stars Jen Brister and Darren Harriott(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Katie Razzall(n)

programme.title: Universal Credit: Inside the Welfare State
programme.desc: Documentary exploring Universal Credit from the point of view of both government employees and those who rely on the controversial benefits system to support themselves. The first episode focuses on Peckham Jobcentre in London. Rachel, who left her job to care for her elderly parents, now struggles to support her family on Universal Credit while having to cope with debt and depression. Jobcentre employee Karen finds herself faced with similar difficulties to her clients, and has to take a second job to support herself. The programme also follows the difficulties faced by civil servants in overseeing sweeping changes to the benefits system(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: A look at some of the funding issues for parliamentary candidates. Plus, the Government's independent enquiry into sex abuse. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP heads to Europe to discover how the divisions exposed by Brexit are reflected across the EU, exploring the role that national identity and pride have had to play in fuelling the rise of right-wing populism across the continent. He begins in the Netherlands, and the fishing towns of Den Helder and Scheveningen, where he meets a veteran fisherman who explains how EU rules and regulations have made the job harder(n)

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Anna Larkin thinks Australia will offer her son the perfect lifestyle, but husband Aaron is moving nowhere until they have spent a trial week in Brisbane. With Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin-Sharp and Raj Bisram continue their tour of Kent as they search for items to sell at an auction in Colchester(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Armed with an interwar Bradshaw's guide, Michael Portillo explores the Britain of the 1930s as he sets out on another adventure. Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation that led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the government for work. The first leg of his journey ends Spennymoor, Co Durham, where he meets the son of a miner who became one of the most famous 20th-century artists of the region(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In this six-part series, the chef Tom Kerridge and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating lower calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months(n)

programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: A woman hoping to rent a property in Goa, India, to share with her son and his girlfriend, with the assistance of local expert Yshana(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: The team examines stories related to the sea. Novice sailor Nick Crane enters the annual one-day yacht race around the Isle of Wight. In Milford Haven, Pembrokeshire, Ruth Goodman investigates the story of the Dutch men who adapted their fishing skills to clear mines laid in the seas around British ports during the Second World War and naval historian Nick Hewitt explores the remains of a First World War German U-boat in the mud of the Medway estuary(n)

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A profile of Oscar-winning actress, featuring archived interviews and classic clips revealing highlights from her life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms(n)

programme.title: Yentl (1983)
programme.desc: A Jewish woman is left alone in the world after the death of her father, who had been secretly teaching her Talmud law in defiance of religious tradition. To continue her education, she disguises herself as a man, but ends up being pushed into an arranged marriage to another woman. Musical drama, directed by and starring Barbra Streisand. With Mandy Patinkin and Amy Irving(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents an edition from Bristol Cathedral. Natasha Raskin Sharp notices a painting of an apothecary on a piece of tin, and James Lewis looks at a valuable royal ring(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Maggie Aderin-Pocock, the Rev Richard Coles, Stuart Maconie and Lou Sanders test their skills in a variety of trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo tours the imperial city of Hue, crosses the former demilitarized zone, which once separated North from South Vietnam, and takes a boat ride on the Red River Delta(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Broadcaster Mariella Frostrup and her friend, the fashion and interior designer Pearl Lowe, go searching for antiques in their adopted county of Somerset. Both are paired with personal shoppers offering sage guidance - dealer Raj Bisram and auctioneer James Braxton. The items that catch their eyes include Norwegian jewellery, African sculpture and some traditional English furniture. But who will haggle the hardest when it comes to making a deal?(n)

programme.title: Secrets of the Museum
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the world-famous Victoria and Albert Museum in London. Only a small amount of the two million items in the V&A's collection are on display to the public. This series gets a glimpse at the treasure trove kept behind closed doors and follows the experts and conservators as they breathe new life into some of these fragile items, such as Pumpie the 100-year-old toy elephant, who needs urgent TLC, and the Christian Dior gowns that get a new lease of life(n)

programme.title: Travels in Euroland with Ed Balls
programme.desc: The former MP travels to Italy and France, finding out how years of austerity have affected politics across Europe. In Italy, he compares the lives of multimillionaire fashion entrepreneur Valeria Marini, who lives in Italy's wealthiest city, Milan, and a farming family from Calabria, one of the poorest regions. Ed ends his trip in France, where Marine le Pen has increased the vote for her rebranded National Rally party, in part by promising to protect French industry from foreign traders. He meets voters unsure which way to turn and asks what the future holds for European politics(n)

programme.title: Mum
programme.desc: After a night out at a restaurant, Pauline has had too much to drink. Meanwhile, Jason has something he needs to say to Cathy. Starring Lesley Manville, Sam Swainsbury, Dorothy Atkinson and Peter Mullan(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: The couples are split up for a navigation challenge that pits men against women on a trek through the wilderness. A local search and rescue expert oversees their efforts and offers his thoughts on which couple should be the next to be eliminated from the contest. After another pair has gone home, the remaining competitors are taken to a gun range to be introduced to the Alaskan attitude to firearms and put their marksmanship to the test(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Botticelli's Venus: The Making of an Icon
programme.desc: Sam Roddick explores the enduring appeal of 15th-century painting The Birth of Venus, revealing its journey to worldwide fame was far from straightforward after lying in obscurity for centuries. He examines why Botticelli's nude was so revolutionary, and looks at its impact on modern culture with artists including Terry Gilliam, who memorably re-invented it for his Monty Python's Flying Circus animations(n)

programme.title: Life Drawing Live!
programme.desc: Josie d'Arby hosts a live broadcast of a life drawing class led by artists Daphne Todd and Lachlan Goudie, offering viewers the chance to draw along with the six amateur artists in the studio as they capture a series of poses. Plus, artist and teacher Diana Ali offers some helpful tips(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna reaches the end of her tether and tries to drive bulimic Delilah out of the house. Warren antagonises Miles by telling him to take an Aids test and Egg feigns injury to evade an increasingly frustrated Milly's amorous advances(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Miles urges Delilah to have an HIV test and Egg, deeply embarrassed at having caused a scene, seeks advice from Warren's therapist. Anna worries she isn't getting enough work at the chambers and hits on the bright idea of using her feminine charms to drum up business(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna organises a special dinner to fan the flickering flame of romance between Milly and Egg, while Miles gets short shrift from his father. Meanwhile, Warren fears blabbermouth cousin Kira will discover he's gay and spill the beans to the rest of the family. Jason Hughes, Jack Davenport and Daniela Nardini star(n)

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby investigates the importance of the sea to trade and to the individual livelihoods of coastal communities. He sails along the west coast of Scotland, starting at Craobh Haven and along the Crinan Canal, a waterway designed by East Lothian engineer John Rennie to take 120 miles off the journey from the west coast to Glasgow and safely avoid the turbulent waters around the Mull of Kintyre. He also climbs aboard the last working sea-going Clyde puffer, where he earns his keep shovelling coal in the steam room, and reflects on the role played by the river in both trade and shipbuilding(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: The return of the documentary about the vibrant world of Irish dance, beginning as master tapper Chris Naish follows up on the success of his online exhibitions by challenging himself to put together a new dance crew to shoot another video within the space of a week(n)

programme.title: Jigs & Wigs
programme.desc: The Zephaniah Dance Ministry comes together in Portadown to spread the word of the gospels through Irish dance, under the guidance of teacher Deborah Anderson(n)

programme.title: Carved with Love: The Genius of British Woodwork
programme.desc: Paul Copley narrates the story of Grinling Gibbons, the 17th-century woodcarver who helped restore London to its former glory in the aftermath of the Great Fire. He reveals how Gibbons' masterpieces - created for the likes of Charles II and William of Orange - were held in such high regard he came to be known as 'the Michelangelo of wood'(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Documentary using clips from the BBC archives to explore how six decades of TV has influenced the understanding of the arts. In the first edition, Kate Bryan looks at the use of the nude by both artists and film-makers as a means to shock their audiences and challenge conventions(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: The Beauty of Anatomy
programme.desc: First in a five-part series in which Dr Adam Rutherford investigates the close relationship between discoveries in anatomy and the works of art that illustrate them. He begins by focusing on Roman anatomist Claudius Galen and artist and part-time dissector Leonardo da Vinci(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani and Robert Llewellyn go behind the scenes at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus to find out what role it plays in long distance travel across India. The station team have to service trains that can be more than 500 metres in length and Robert joins them to watch the quick turnaround in action. Dan goes behind the scenes with the teams who process more than 25,000 dirty sheets a day and ensure that everyone on board is fed and watered, while Anita takes a long distance sleeper across India to discover who is travellling and what the conditions are like on board. Plus, a look at one of the country's poshest trains, which costs £600 per night(n)

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Andrew Martin investigates why named trains, once the pride of the railway companies that ran them, have now largely been confined to locomotive history. Embarking on a series of rail journeys around Britain, Andrew revisits the routes travelled by the Flying Scotsman, the Cornish Riviera Express and the Brighton Belle, three of Britain's most famous named trains, as he reflects on the bygone golden age of railways(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Anna gets drunk and seduces Jo, who immediately convinces himself he's in love with her. Warren agonises over whether to tell brother Dale he's gay, and Egg resists Milly's attempts to find him a job, deciding to write a novel instead(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Egg gets a visit from his father, who is bearing bad news, while Anna makes a spectacle of herself at Hooperman's party, and Warren decides he must get his priorities straight. Drama, starring Andrew Lincoln, Daniela Nardini and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: This Life
programme.desc: Warren's new-found lover calls it quits in a dramatic turn of events, while Egg's father provides a constant reminder of his own inadequacies. Only Anna sees through Jerry's self-pity and tells him to buck up his ideas - with surprising results. Drama, starring Jason Hughes, Paul Copley and Daniela Nardini(n)

programme.title: Art, Passion & Power: The Story of the Royal Collection
programme.desc: Andrew Graham-Dixon tells the story of the Royal Collection's remarkable resurrection. Following its fortunes from Charles II through to the 18th century and the enlightened collecting of George III, cameras reveal some of its greatest treasures, including a jewel-encrusted salt cellar in the shape of a castle and a gold State Coach(n)

programme.title: Britain and the Sea
programme.desc: David Dimbleby sails along the coast of East Anglia, exploring how Britain's view of the sea has changed over the past 200 years. The coast and the beach became a playground for the nation, inspiring artists, transforming coastal architecture and creating a seaside culture that remains uniquely British. Setting out from Gorleston-on-Sea, he travels down the Suffolk and Essex coasts and into the Thames, until he reaches Greenwich. Along the way he discovers works of art that reflect Britain's evolving relationship with the sea, meets a sand sculptor and visits the Queen's House in Greenwich, built in 1616 as a waterside retreat for Queen Anne and now home to Britain's greatest collection of maritime artefacts(n)

programme.title: World's Busiest Railway 2015
programme.desc: Dan Snow, Anita Rani and Robert Llewellyn go behind the scenes at Mumbai's Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus to find out what role it plays in long distance travel across India. programme.title: The Pennine Way
programme.desc: Paul Rose's continuing journey takes him into the Yorkshire Dales, where 50 years ago the Pennine Way was launched in the village of Malham. Paul hears from those who remember the opening ceremony, before pulling on his climbing gear to attempt the limestone cliff face of Malham Cove. He also visits a sheep sale in Wensleydale and enjoys a pint in Britain's highest pub(n)

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how gravity influences events across the universe, discovering why such a relatively weak force dictates Earth's orbit around the Sun - as well as the solar system's movement through the galaxy. He also explains why the gravity of a neutron star is much stronger than that of Earth, and looks back through history to reveal why scientists' research into the power of attraction has given mankind a far greater understanding of the cosmos(n)

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Today we meet a new family, five-year-old twins Daisy and Lily. They live near the seaside with their mum, dad and younger twin sisters Molly and Nell. The family has a busy breakfast time with all those mouths to feed, and Daisy and Lily are in charge of counting the eggs. Later, Mum takes the girls out for some fun on the beach where they make a picture using pebbles and seaweed. Back at home Daisy and Lily get to work in their toy kitchen, making pretend soup for all the family. But will their sausage roll and vegetable soup be a success at their super-soup party?(n)

programme.title: Swashbuckle
programme.desc: Gem and her teams of Swashbucklers try to win her jewels back from pirates Cook, Line and Sinker. Game show for children(n)

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The squirrels are playing Copy the King when Tag falls over and bumps his knee. Luckily, Duggee has his First Aid badge, so he can bandage Tag up(n)

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: Kwazii yearns after some treasure in a sunken pirate ship, but discovers it is protected by slime-squirting slime eels(n)

programme.title: Andy's Safari Adventures
programme.desc: Andy heads to the Caribbean in search of some seagrass he needs for a rescued manatee.(n)

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie looks at clothes. She uses a special microscope camera to look at the zip on her hoodie. An animation shows how the slider works to pull the two rows of teeth together to close the zip. Next, Maddie visits a workshop to see how a dinosaur picture is screen printed onto a t-shirt. Her special microscope camera shows how a stencil, created on a screen, allows the ink to pass through and create the design(n)

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.desc: When Sammy Whiskers and Mr Tod gatecrash Jeremy Fisher's annual lakeside get-together, it is up to Peter to save the party(n)

programme.title: Go Jetters
programme.desc: Cartoon following four adventure-seeking superheroes as they explore places all around the world(n)

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: Comedy sketch show for younger viewers. Will Singalot sings as Robin Hood chases a runaway goat, and there's a surprise in store for Mr Macdonald when Dina Lady and Tommy arrive with a chocolate cake.(n)

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Doug's tooth has fallen out, so Jess and Simon tell Waffle about the Tooth Fairy. Waffle longs to meet the Tooth Fairy and his dream comes true(n)

programme.title: Molly and Mack
programme.desc: Mack buys Daisy a puppy called Little Mo. Moira says no pets are allowed in the Big Hub, so Molly and Mack try to hide Little Mo, but then she escapes(n)

programme.title: Moon and Me
programme.desc: Moon Baby, Pepi Nana and their friends discover a toy roundabout left in the Toy House(n)

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Small is upset when, while out fishing in the music boat with Major Clanger, he misses seeing two spectacular meteors streaking across the sky. Narrated by Michael Palin(n)

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Pontipines and Wottingers join Makka Pakka, Upsy Daisy and Igglepiggle for a bouncy ride on board the Ninky Nonk(n)

programme.title: CBeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Actress Ashley Jensen reads Paper Dolls, by Julia Donaldson and Rebecca Cobb(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: An audacious scam robs NHS hospitals of more than £12.5million and a hospital cashier leads the high life, taking money meant for patients and putting it into her own bank account(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell, Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin meet property developers who tell their stories of refurbishment projects in Darwen, Lancashire, Bolton and Levenshulme(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: X-Ray
programme.desc: Rachel Treadaway-Williams investigates a rogue trader who is a familiar face on X-Ray, this time selling new roofs for conservatories - and letting down customers again. Lucy Owen is on a mission to find out why her recycling bags are being rejected by her local waste recycling plant and Omar Hamdi finds out how one internet provider appears to have revived the practice of slamming, where customers are switched from one company to another without their knowledge or consent(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and experts Jonathan Pratt and Danny Sebastian travel to Kedleston Hall in Derbyshire for the buying and selling contest. Two married couples go head to head to buy three items that will hopefully make a profit at auction, and they all have their sights set on a golden gavel. Eric also visits the small village of Eyam to discover how, 350 years ago, the villagers made a daring decision to quarantine themselves, and stop the spread of the plague(n)

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al tries to cure Bear of his fear of blood, as Daniel and Zara continue to work through their troubled past. On a drug raid, Rob bumps into two familiar faces(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu take on the case of a washed-up rock star who believes that the Devil is trying to kill him, and set out to unmask the real culprit(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Alistair Appleton helps a couple from Worcestershire to find a home in Cornwall, and discovers a project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Sheep farmers Ian and Fiona compete against the Wills family, who run a mixed farm and exhibit pigs, at the Yeovil Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp and Raj Bisram look for bargains in East Anglia, as well as having a go at step dancing and discovering an 18th-century collection of self-playing instruments(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Matt Baker present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Whitney is racked with guilt about Kush and plans to put things right, despite Tiffany urging her not to do anything that puts her in danger. Shirley is determined to make Linda face her problems and pleads with Mick to take his wife to an addict support session at the community centre. Ash and her brothers are surprised by Suki's reaction to Iqra, Sheree opens up to Patrick about Isaac, and Sharon makes a peace offering to Dennis(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day Live: The FA Cup
programme.desc: Oxford United v Newcastle United (Kick-off 8.05pm). Coverage of the fourth-round replay, which comes from Kassam Stadium, as the teams contest the right to face West Bromwich Albion at The Hawthorns in the next round. The match at St James' Park ended 0-0, with Newcastle forward Joelinton having two goals disallowed for offside, and League One Oxford will now feel they have an excellent chance of producing an upset against their Premier League opponents(n)

programme.title: Plastic Surgery Undressed
programme.desc: Vogue Williams and Mobeen Azhar explain the ins and outs of liposuction, and offer four people considering the procedure a sneak preview of exactly what it entails, by watching a surgeon operate on a sportsman whose physique has suffered following an injury. The very different experiences of two women who have undergone liposuction brings the risks and rewards of the process to light(n)

programme.title: Easy Ways to Live Well
programme.desc: Hugh Fearnley-Whittingstall and Steph McGovern challenge staff at a garden centre in the Wirral to test remedies for their aches and pains. The pair also tackle their own health concerns, including Steph's apprehension at exercising while pregnant and Hugh's worry that he's starting to lose his mental sharpness. Hugh then meets a family in Devon struggling with a very common ailment - fussy eating. With mealtimes a battlefield, he wants to discover if a counterintuitive idea may finally bring peace to the dinner table(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Fraud Squad NHS
programme.desc: Investigators are on the case of a locksmith who conned a London hospital out of almost half a million pounds by over-charging for supplies(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and the team explore properties about to be auctioned in Crewe, Cheshire, Blackwell in the West Midlands, and Poplar in east London(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Anna Larkin thinks Australia will offer her son the perfect lifestyle, but husband Aaron is moving nowhere until they have spent a trial week in Brisbane. With Nicki Chapman(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: The team catch up with a second-hand car dealer who sold a potentially deadly vehicle, and track down a fish merchant who has been illegally harvesting shellfish(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Paul Laidlaw and Caroline Hawley help two teams look for items at Newark Antiques Fair in Nottinghamshire, before seeing if they make profits at auction. Anita Manning presents(n)

programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Medical drama series set in a busy Midlands surgery. Daniel attends a restaurant run by prison inmates and has to help chaplain Cathy overcome her crisis of faith.(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders who are being threatened with eviction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman heads to Cornwall on a property search with a couple who have £750,000 to realise their dream of starting their married life together near the Cornish coast. While in the area, Nicki visits Looe Harbour to learn about the importance of sustainable fishing methods, which is championed by the local fishmongers she meets(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Flower farmer Shelley Sishton and sweet pea grower Darren Everest compete at the Kent County Show. Their displays are bespoke and spectacular as they go all out for a gold card(n)

programme.desc: Natasha Raskin-Sharp and Raj Bisram continue their tour of Kent as they search for items to sell at an auction in Colchester(n)
